context: Stage B first live probe collapse-detector schema mismatch
failure: RuntimeError: eval_summary: required metric 'direct_answer_substitution_count' not found in summary

options.label: Add metric emission to canonical eval summary
options.summary: Extend canonical evaluator output schema to emit old-style failure_profile metrics (or equivalent typed metrics) needed by threshold profile.
advantages: Long-term alignment between evaluator and detector
disadvantages: Does not repair already-produced summary unless eval is rerun or a trusted post-hoc reconstruction is added
Requires evaluator code change and regression validation

options.label: Update detector metric mapping to current eval-summary fields
options.summary: Map required metrics directly to current adapter/base aggregate/per_split fields.
advantages: Can use existing eval summary immediately for metrics that do exist
disadvantages: Several required metrics have no strict equivalent (direct_answer_substitution_count, no_anchor_exact_valid_share, read_file_symbol_name_exact_valid_rate)
Proxy mapping can silently alter scientific meaning
options.risk_hiding_real_failure: medium_to_high if semantic proxies replace missing metrics

options.label: Update threshold profile to reference available metrics
options.summary: Retarget threshold profile metric_catalog and rules to fields present in current eval summary.
advantages: Can allow immediate detector run against existing summary
disadvantages: Changes governance contract and thresholds semantics
Dropping/replacing unavailable metrics may weaken intended safeguards

options.label: Add explicit metric alias mapping
options.summary: Add a typed alias layer in detector so old metric IDs can resolve to new schema paths when semantically equivalent.
advantages: Can recover many metrics without changing eval outputs
disadvantages: Still cannot resolve metrics that have no semantic equivalent in current summary
Needs careful alias governance and versioning
options.risk_hiding_real_failure: low_to_medium if aliases are strict and documented
options.allows_detector_on_existing_summary_with: partial (full only with noncomputable handling for unmatched metrics)

options.label: Mark metric non-computable when absent, governance-safe
options.summary: Change detector missing-metric behavior from fail-fast runtime exception to per-rule noncomputable accounting with explicit status handling.
advantages: Detector can run immediately on existing summary
With current profile, noncomputable_status=halt_progression preserves safety
disadvantages: Outputs are less informative until schema alignment is completed
Requires clear reporting to avoid false sense of pass
options.risk_hiding_real_failure: low (if noncomputable forces halt/watch as configured)

cross_cutting_observation.baseline_schema_ambiguity: wrapper_leakage_overall currently resolves ambiguously on baseline under existing candidate_paths
cross_cutting_observation.implication: Any repair should also disambiguate wrapper leakage metric resolution to avoid next failure after direct-answer fix
